mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead economic activity by 2-3 months through supply chains and construction employment. A sustained high level of housing starts indicates robust demand that translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year rank IC of HOUST on forward 5-day returns remains negative or below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% in a tightening cycle, it raises the discount rate for equity valuations and increases borrowing costs, compressing corporate margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, the average forward 20-day return is not statistically significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over all such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of very low unemployment (UNRATE ≤ 5.0%) and high employment (PAYEMS above 134,000) signals a tight labor market that drives wage inflation, eroding corporate profit margins and leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal does not produce a negative mean return across all occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime defined by CPI YoY above 3%, any increase in the Fed funds rate (above a 25bp threshold) reinforces tightening expectations and reduces the present value of future earnings, causing negative forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS change >= 0.25 AND CPI YoY > 3% (estimated from CPIAUCSL)
failure_condition: In regimes with CPI YoY > 3%, the average forward 20-day return following a 25bp+ rate increase is not negative, or the signal does not outperform a always-negative benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is a coincident indicator of aggregate output. A consistently rising index signals strong manufacturing activity and economic momentum, which supports corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The rank IC of INDPRO (monthly change) on forward 60-day returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) exceed $350 billion, it indicates strong consumer spending that directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive short-term equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: In months following RSAFS > 350000, the average forward 5-day S&P 500 return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Employment (PAYEMS) is a robust indicator of economic health. A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls signals increasing aggregate income and demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a month.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly PAYEMS change and forward 20-day SPX return is not positive over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed is actively tightening (FEDFUNDS > 4%) and industrial production starts to decline (2 consecutive monthly drops in INDPRO), it signals both cost of capital increase and weakening economic activity, creating a negative pressure on equities over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4% AND INDPRO declines for 2 months
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the average forward 20-day return is greater than -0.5% or the signal fails to produce a negative mean return in out-of-sample testing.
